real_questions.text: Do you maintain leverage below your defined ceiling under stress, and are your asset coverage cushions sufficient to absorb four quarters of maximum redemptions and valuation declines simultaneously?
Your leverage is running against your target debt-to-tangible-equity ratio — walk us through the parent's capacity and commitment to inject capital if you exceed it.

common_pitfalls.text: Preparing for a single-quarter shock — Fitch's BDC stress construct is a sustained four-quarter drawdown of maximum redemptions and valuation declines at the same time.

Treating the leverage conversation as standalone — Fitch's captive framework assumes a hypothetical parent capital injection back to target when actual leverage exceeds it, so the credibility and capacity of parent support IS the conversation.

strong_answer_markers.text: Has the four-quarter redemption + valuation-decline stress pre-run, with leverage and asset coverage results ready to show.
Speaks to parent support capacity and credibility alongside standalone leverage, knowing Fitch's model assumes the injection.

agency_intel.text: Fitch's captive finance framework sets a credit-profile target leverage ratio (debt-to-tangible-equity) per captive, calibrated to asset quality and the funding/liquidity profile. When actual leverage exceeds target, Fitch assumes the parent injects a hypothetical amount of capital to bring leverage back to target.
